item.title: 4th Annual Classic Car Show Returns to St. John’s This Sunday

media:content.media:description: Vintage Cars at the Healthcare Foundation Fundraiser

item.dc:creator: Beth Fagan
item.description:
Ack b'ys, they're havin' that classic car show again at the Dr. L. A. Miller Centre in St. John's tomorrow. 'Tis the fourth one, eh? The whole shindig's raisin' money for the Health Care Foundation's Patient Enrichment Fund, which means it'll help folks who are gettin' rehab there feel a tad more comfy.
Expect more than 80 vintage beauties; it’s like takin' a trip down memory lane for the ol’ timers. And sure, there's gonna be grub, a BBQ, and prize draws up the yin-yang from 12 to 3 p.m. Best part? Walkin' in is free as a breeze, but any money you dish out on food and such goes straight to patient programs. So, give it a shot if you're into cars or just need an excuse to escape the missus for an afternoon!
item.content:encoded:
Ack b'ys, they're havin' that classic car show again at the Dr. L. A. Miller Centre in St. John's tomorrow. 'Tis the fourth one, eh? The whole shindig's raisin' money for the Health Care Foundation's Patient Enrichment Fund, which means it'll help folks who are gettin' rehab there feel a tad more comfy.
Expect more than 80 vintage beauties; it’s like takin' a trip down memory lane for the ol’ timers. And sure, there's gonna be grub, a BBQ, and prize draws up the yin-yang from 12 to 3 p.m. Best part? Walkin' in is free as a breeze, but any money you dish out on food and such goes straight to patient programs. So, give it a shot if you're into cars or just need an excuse to escape the missus for an afternoon!

item.title: Harvester Loan Program Revised to Better Support Local Fishers
item.dc:creator: Beth Fagan
item.description:
Well b'ys, looks like the government's been busy tinkering with that Harvester Enterprise Loan Program again. They're tryin' to make it easier for fishers to get their mitts on some loans. Instead of bundling everything together like cod and capelin in a net, they're splitting up the down payment loans and interest rebates so folks can grab 'em separately, if they fancy. And if you don't want the whole guarantee business attached, you can still dive in for them other perks of the program.
They’re still offering up to $4-million in guarantees to banks if they're being finicky about lending out money, so that's still there if need be. Government's patting themselves on the back, saying it’s all peachy – $3.7 million in loan guarantees and more money in down payment loans and rebates dished out. But for some of us, seems like they just made the paperwork more of a tangled mess.
Anyway, if you’re keen to read the gov’s take on it, check it out on their site. Just be ready for some bedtime reading that might put ya to sleep faster than the sound of the waves at the cabin.
item.content:encoded:
Well b'ys, looks like the government's been busy tinkering with that Harvester Enterprise Loan Program again. They're tryin' to make it easier for fishers to get their mitts on some loans. Instead of bundling everything together like cod and capelin in a net, they're splitting up the down payment loans and interest rebates so folks can grab 'em separately, if they fancy. And if you don't want the whole guarantee business attached, you can still dive in for them other perks of the program.
They’re still offering up to $4-million in guarantees to banks if they're being finicky about lending out money, so that's still there if need be. Government's patting themselves on the back, saying it’s all peachy – $3.7 million in loan guarantees and more money in down payment loans and rebates dished out. But for some of us, seems like they just made the paperwork more of a tangled mess.
Anyway, if you’re keen to read the gov’s take on it, check it out on their site. Just be ready for some bedtime reading that might put ya to sleep faster than the sound of the waves at the cabin.

item.title: Contract Awarded for New K-12 School in Cartwright
item.dc:creator: Beth Fagan
item.description:
Well, b'ys, it seems like the ol' Henry Gordon Academy in Cartwright is finally getting the boot after looking like a puzzle missing half its pieces for God knows how long. Lisa Dempster, the MHA, said the place is about as useful as a chocolate teapot, especially when it comes to accessibility. She remembers the poor young fella with a disability navigating those treacherous steps like it was some sort of obstacle course.
The government's decided to fork out $38.4 million to build a new school, throw in some teacher housing, and give the old building the heave-ho. They'll even slap down a new parking lot and sports field. Maybe now the youngins won't have to learn in a place that looks like it was designed by some blind dogs. It'll be nice to see something decent in Cartwright, if it all comes together without hitches—though, with the cost of things these days, $38 million might just buy a fancy outhouse and some new nails.
item.content:encoded:
Well, b'ys, it seems like the ol' Henry Gordon Academy in Cartwright is finally getting the boot after looking like a puzzle missing half its pieces for God knows how long. Lisa Dempster, the MHA, said the place is about as useful as a chocolate teapot, especially when it comes to accessibility. She remembers the poor young fella with a disability navigating those treacherous steps like it was some sort of obstacle course.
The government's decided to fork out $38.4 million to build a new school, throw in some teacher housing, and give the old building the heave-ho. They'll even slap down a new parking lot and sports field. Maybe now the youngins won't have to learn in a place that looks like it was designed by some blind dogs. It'll be nice to see something decent in Cartwright, if it all comes together without hitches—though, with the cost of things these days, $38 million might just buy a fancy outhouse and some new nails.

item.title: MHA Criticizes Income Limit for New Medical Travel Assistance
item.dc:creator: Beth Fagan
item.description:
Well, my ducky, here’s the scoop: The government's after rollin' out this new pilot program with Hope Air that’s supposed to give folks travel and lodging for medical appointments if yer household makes $60,000 or less. Sounds grand, right? But here’s where it gets sticky.
Lela Evans, an MHA from Torngat Mountains, is raisin' quite the ruckus about it. She’s sayin' it's gone and created more of them barriers to healthcare, especially up in Labrador. She's callin’ it a “tiered system,” which is just fancy talk for makin’ it harder for folks.
Now, what gets me goat is what they’re callin’ “moderate” income. $60,000 for a household? In Labrador? Might as well be tryin' to buy a pint with a penny—that ain't gettin’ anyone far when livin' costs a fortune up there. If you've got a young fella or lass tryin’ to help out but still livin' at home 'cause they can't afford their own place, it just messes with the numbers. How 'bouts they try livin' down here on that and see how it goes, eh? Seems like they're makin’ it all a bit more complicated than it needs to be.
item.content:encoded:
Well, my ducky, here’s the scoop: The government's after rollin' out this new pilot program with Hope Air that’s supposed to give folks travel and lodging for medical appointments if yer household makes $60,000 or less. Sounds grand, right? But here’s where it gets sticky.
Lela Evans, an MHA from Torngat Mountains, is raisin' quite the ruckus about it. She’s sayin' it's gone and created more of them barriers to healthcare, especially up in Labrador. She's callin’ it a “tiered system,” which is just fancy talk for makin’ it harder for folks.
Now, what gets me goat is what they’re callin’ “moderate” income. $60,000 for a household? In Labrador? Might as well be tryin' to buy a pint with a penny—that ain't gettin’ anyone far when livin' costs a fortune up there. If you've got a young fella or lass tryin’ to help out but still livin' at home 'cause they can't afford their own place, it just messes with the numbers. How 'bouts they try livin' down here on that and see how it goes, eh? Seems like they're makin’ it all a bit more complicated than it needs to be.

item.title: One Seriously Injured After Moose-Vehicle Collision Near Whitbourne
item.dc:creator: Beth Fagan
item.description:
Oh me nerves, another moose-vehicle crash, b'y! This young fella's in serious condition after tangling with a moose on the TCH near Whitbourne, poor soul. Happened right after dark, 'cause apparently the moose decided to start their own rush hour or somethin'. There was another person in the car too, but thank goodness they're not as banged up.
Now, the Whitbourne Fire Chief, Jamie Budden, is saying we had a trio of these moose meet-ups just last night between nine and midnight. Seems like the moose are on a road trip of their own these days.
Budden's tellin' folks to "think moose" while they're drivin', whatever that means. Might as well ask folks to "think fish" when they're on the ocean! Anyways, he's seen more of these moose showin' up on the highways lately, like they're auditioning for a role in The Fast and the Furious: Newfoundland Drift.
In addition to the three bum-byes from last night, we had a couple more in the past week or so. He's askin' folks to slow down, especially at night. So, folks, if you're out on the road, keep yer eyes peeled and don’t forget, if there's one moose, there's probably more — they aren't loners, that's for sure!
item.content:encoded:
Oh me nerves, another moose-vehicle crash, b'y! This young fella's in serious condition after tangling with a moose on the TCH near Whitbourne, poor soul. Happened right after dark, 'cause apparently the moose decided to start their own rush hour or somethin'. There was another person in the car too, but thank goodness they're not as banged up.
Now, the Whitbourne Fire Chief, Jamie Budden, is saying we had a trio of these moose meet-ups just last night between nine and midnight. Seems like the moose are on a road trip of their own these days.
Budden's tellin' folks to "think moose" while they're drivin', whatever that means. Might as well ask folks to "think fish" when they're on the ocean! Anyways, he's seen more of these moose showin' up on the highways lately, like they're auditioning for a role in The Fast and the Furious: Newfoundland Drift.
In addition to the three bum-byes from last night, we had a couple more in the past week or so. He's askin' folks to slow down, especially at night. So, folks, if you're out on the road, keep yer eyes peeled and don’t forget, if there's one moose, there's probably more — they aren't loners, that's for sure!

item.title: Fundraiser Launched for Local Rocker Liz Solo
item.dc:creator: Beth Fagan
item.description:
Ah, buddy, 'tis a rough tumble for sure. So, this local musician, Liz Pickard—everyone calls her Liz Solo—went an' had a nasty fall that broke her leg and done a number on her ligaments and whatnot. Poor thing can't even walk at the moment, and she's got herself a long stretch of rehab ahead.
But here's the kicker: folks are rallyin' round, like a kitchen party with a purpose, to help her out with the expenses. Her best buddy, Colleen Power, says Liz is a bit of a legend 'round these parts—a "pioneer female rocker," if you will. Apparently, she's been the go-to gal for benefits and gigs whenever anyone needed a hand, helpin' out more folks than you'd fit in a dory on a good day. So now, it's our turn to return the favour and give her a bit of support.
If ya want more details on chuckin' in a few bucks, you can check out the fundraiser page. As they say, it's always good to give a lil' back to the ones who've given so much. Now let's just hope she gets back on her feet soon and not end up like one of them codfish stuck outta the water!
item.content:encoded:
Ah, buddy, 'tis a rough tumble for sure. So, this local musician, Liz Pickard—everyone calls her Liz Solo—went an' had a nasty fall that broke her leg and done a number on her ligaments and whatnot. Poor thing can't even walk at the moment, and she's got herself a long stretch of rehab ahead.
But here's the kicker: folks are rallyin' round, like a kitchen party with a purpose, to help her out with the expenses. Her best buddy, Colleen Power, says Liz is a bit of a legend 'round these parts—a "pioneer female rocker," if you will. Apparently, she's been the go-to gal for benefits and gigs whenever anyone needed a hand, helpin' out more folks than you'd fit in a dory on a good day. So now, it's our turn to return the favour and give her a bit of support.
If ya want more details on chuckin' in a few bucks, you can check out the fundraiser page. As they say, it's always good to give a lil' back to the ones who've given so much. Now let's just hope she gets back on her feet soon and not end up like one of them codfish stuck outta the water!

item.title: Police Warn of Counterfeit Money Circulating on West Coast

media:content.media:description: PiggyBank / Unsplash

item.dc:creator: Beth Fagan
item.description:
Oh b'y, listen to this foolishness. Turns out some folks are out on the west coast passin' 'round funny money like it’s some kinda Monopoly game. The RNC got a call ’cause someone used a bogus $100 bill to pay for grub at a joint on Maple Valley Road. Can you imagine? They must be thinkin' they're slick.
Meanwhile, the Bay St. George RCMP are hearin' tales of fake twenties and hundreds bein’ used on Facebook Marketplace and at local shops. These counterfeit bills all got the same serial number and, get this, the fancy reflective portrait is just a sticker you can peel off. You gotta wonder who they think they're foolin'.
The cops are tellin' businesses to keep their eyes peeled and check every bit of money that comes their way. If you notice anything iffy, you're supposed to give the RNC, RCMP, or Crime Stoppers a shout. I tell ya, some people must think we’re hard up for cash and a bit gullible to fall for this racket!
item.content:encoded:
Oh b'y, listen to this foolishness. Turns out some folks are out on the west coast passin' 'round funny money like it’s some kinda Monopoly game. The RNC got a call ’cause someone used a bogus $100 bill to pay for grub at a joint on Maple Valley Road. Can you imagine? They must be thinkin' they're slick.
Meanwhile, the Bay St. George RCMP are hearin' tales of fake twenties and hundreds bein’ used on Facebook Marketplace and at local shops. These counterfeit bills all got the same serial number and, get this, the fancy reflective portrait is just a sticker you can peel off. You gotta wonder who they think they're foolin'.
The cops are tellin' businesses to keep their eyes peeled and check every bit of money that comes their way. If you notice anything iffy, you're supposed to give the RNC, RCMP, or Crime Stoppers a shout. I tell ya, some people must think we’re hard up for cash and a bit gullible to fall for this racket!

item.title: Best Kind BBQ Kicks Off Today in Mount Pearl
item.dc:creator: Beth Fagan
item.description:
Well b'y, let me tell ya what's up. The Best Kind BBQ is kickin' off today over in St. David's Park, Mount Pearl. It's turned into quite the thing, eh? They've got yer tunes, grub, artsy stuff, and some games to keep everyone from noddin' off. All in all, it's just a big ol' whoop-up for the summer around these parts.
Now, this Brent Mac fella who's runnin' the show, says it started off as a music festival called the Random Sound Music Festival. But, wouldn't ya know it, they decided to jazz it up with some bells and whistles. In 2017, they settled on the whole "Best Kind BBQ" angle. And by the time Mount Pearl caught wind of it, they were onboard faster than ya can say "hold onto yer hat!" So since 2019, it's been a yearly bash at St. David's Park.
Can't say I'm surprised the city hopped on, 'specially since it's turned into such a grand affair. One thing's for sure, at this rate, there won't be a hungry or bored soul left in Mount Pearl!
item.content:encoded:
Well b'y, let me tell ya what's up. The Best Kind BBQ is kickin' off today over in St. David's Park, Mount Pearl. It's turned into quite the thing, eh? They've got yer tunes, grub, artsy stuff, and some games to keep everyone from noddin' off. All in all, it's just a big ol' whoop-up for the summer around these parts.
Now, this Brent Mac fella who's runnin' the show, says it started off as a music festival called the Random Sound Music Festival. But, wouldn't ya know it, they decided to jazz it up with some bells and whistles. In 2017, they settled on the whole "Best Kind BBQ" angle. And by the time Mount Pearl caught wind of it, they were onboard faster than ya can say "hold onto yer hat!" So since 2019, it's been a yearly bash at St. David's Park.
Can't say I'm surprised the city hopped on, 'specially since it's turned into such a grand affair. One thing's for sure, at this rate, there won't be a hungry or bored soul left in Mount Pearl!

item.title: Newfoundland and Labrador Sole Holdout on Direct Alcohol Sales Agreement
item.dc:creator: Beth Fagan
item.description:
Well, b’y, it looks like Newfoundland and Labrador is the odd one out again. The Canadian Federation of Independent Business is tellin’ the government to “make up its mind” about whether it’s gonna join the rest of the gang in allowing booze to be sold directly to consumers across provinces. Nine provinces and one territory have already signed on for more free-flowin' libations by next spring, but here we are, sittin’ on our duffs.
The provincial government says it needs a bit more time to figure out what all this hoopla might mean for local businesses and jobs. Suppose you can't rush a good contemplation, eh? But one fella, Louis-Phillipe Gautier, reckons they've had time enough since March to give ‘er a proper think and should be ready to join the others by now.
Honestly, seems to me like they’re dragging their rubber boots through the mud on this one. Might be worried we'll drink all the Screech before they can figure it out!
item.content:encoded:
Well, b’y, it looks like Newfoundland and Labrador is the odd one out again. The Canadian Federation of Independent Business is tellin’ the government to “make up its mind” about whether it’s gonna join the rest of the gang in allowing booze to be sold directly to consumers across provinces. Nine provinces and one territory have already signed on for more free-flowin' libations by next spring, but here we are, sittin’ on our duffs.
The provincial government says it needs a bit more time to figure out what all this hoopla might mean for local businesses and jobs. Suppose you can't rush a good contemplation, eh? But one fella, Louis-Phillipe Gautier, reckons they've had time enough since March to give ‘er a proper think and should be ready to join the others by now.
Honestly, seems to me like they’re dragging their rubber boots through the mud on this one. Might be worried we'll drink all the Screech before they can figure it out!

item.title: New Gordon Pinsent Statue Unveiling Set for This Afternoon
item.dc:creator: Beth Fagan
item.description: "Oh b'y, so they're gittin' on with it, eh? A big ol' statue for Gordon Pinsent's goin' up in Grand Falls-Windsor. 'Bout time, I'd say, considerin' he put us on the map with his actin' for more than 60 years. Passed away he did, poor fella, at 92 this year. They're plannin' to show it off at the town hall at 1:30 p.m. today. Our buddy Fred Hutton, the minister of everythin' but the kitchen sink, will be there too, actin' all official-like. Better hope it don't rain on their parade, but ya know, wouldn't be Newfoundland if it didn't, right?"
item.content:encoded: "Oh b'y, so they're gittin' on with it, eh? A big ol' statue for Gordon Pinsent's goin' up in Grand Falls-Windsor. 'Bout time, I'd say, considerin' he put us on the map with his actin' for more than 60 years. Passed away he did, poor fella, at 92 this year. They're plannin' to show it off at the town hall at 1:30 p.m. today. Our buddy Fred Hutton, the minister of everythin' but the kitchen sink, will be there too, actin' all official-like. Better hope it don't rain on their parade, but ya know, wouldn't be Newfoundland if it didn't, right?"
